articles.title: Netflix expands revamped mobile app across Asia and doubles down on kids’ gaming
articles.description: The media giant is pushing to expand its mobile and gaming business.

articles.title: ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
articles.description: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

articles.title: The Indian government got cold feet on Starlink just before SpaceX’s IPO
articles.description: Problems with Starlink's India expansion could challenge SpaceX's IPO growth story.

articles.title: How memory tools can make AI models worse
articles.description: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

articles.title: Zest launches a restaurant discovery app powered by where people actually eat
articles.description: Backed by Alexis Ohanian’s 776 and Kindred Ventures, Zest uses transaction data and AI to generate restaurant recommendations based on users’ real dining habits and the places they frequent.

articles.title: Cybersecurity researchers aren’t happy about the guardrails on Anthropic’s Fable
articles.description: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has guardrails that are too strict for any cybersecurity work.

articles.title: Why enterprise AI will be a major focus at VivaTech 2026
articles.description: While Silicon Valley continues pushing aggressively into large language models and consumer-facing AI products, many European companies are focused on applying AI to complex systems already embedded into everyday life.

articles.title: Datadog veterans launch AI coding startup Niteshift on a bet against Big AI lock-in
articles.description: AI coding agent startup Niteshift has raised a $7 million seed round from a who's who of angels. It's betting companies will want power over, not lock-in with model makers.

articles.title: The three hard-tech moonshots fueling SpaceX’s unbelievable IPO
articles.description: Most of the value in SpaceX's IPO is effectively a call option on the company's ambitious space data center plans.

articles.title: Pinterest bets on creators with Amazon Storefront integration
articles.description: Pinterest is adding support for Amazon Storefronts, allowing creators to earn affiliate commissions more easily while showcasing their product recommendations in one place.

articles.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
articles.description: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or for training AI models.

articles.title: ServiceNow tells customers a bug left some of their data exposed to the internet
articles.description: ServiceNow is used by thousands of enterprises to automate their internal processes, but says several customers had data accessed because of a security bug.

articles.title: Jedify raises $24M to help companies arm AI agents with context on their business
articles.description: The funding round was led by Norwest, with participation from S Capital VC, Cerca Partners, and Oceans Ventures. Snowflake Ventures also participated as a strategic investor.

articles.title: Decart’s new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
articles.description: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.

articles.title: Snapchat limits users under 16 to sharing Spotlights with friends
articles.description: Users under 16 years old will get a separate profile to show Stories and Spotlight posts to friends that they follow back.

articles.title: Why everyone’s an energy company now
articles.description: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

articles.title: Avalanche’s desktop fusion reactor delivers blistering-hot plasma
articles.description: Fusion power startup Avalanche Energy said its reactor prototype heated a plasma to over 10 million degrees C.

articles.title: Why 2 SpaceX alumni are betting on solar and batteries to power the AI craze
articles.description: Ambrosia Energy wants to build power plants in less than 12 months while undercutting natural gas. It hopes to build gigawatts worth by 2030.

articles.title: Waymo says it built a better benchmark for comparing robotaxis to humans
articles.description: Waymo created a new computer model to help it better understand how humans behave in crash scenarios that its robotaxis encounter.

articles.title: Meta signs first AI data center deal in India with Reliance
articles.description: The 168-megawatt facility will support Meta's global AI computing needs and can be expanded over time.

articles.title: Racist comments targeting politicians tripled since Meta relaxed its rules
articles.description: Violent threats against lawmakers have also surged on Facebook.

articles.title: GM Energy introduces V2G support and new energy storage battery chemistry
articles.description: There are more than a quarter of a million V2G-capable GM EVs on the roads already.

articles.title: Starlink charges $10 monthly hardware fee in move away from one-time purchases
articles.description: Starlink, SpaceX's top moneymaker, also raised service prices by $5 to $10.

articles.title: Locked in heated rivalry with researcher, Microsoft fixes 0-day they disclosed
articles.description: A separate zero-day also disclosed by Nightmare Eclipse appears to be patched as well.

articles.title: Three key vital signs make up the "urban pulse" of a city
articles.description: Cities are dynamic, not static grids, and urbanization is a "spiky," cyclical, and asynchronous process.

articles.title: Commonwealth Fusion makes the physics case for its 400 MW reactor
articles.description: Five peer-reviewed papers update the design and model its expected output.

articles.title: Paramount accuses Netflix of "scorched-earth campaign" against WBD merger
articles.description: Netflix's response: "Absurd."

articles.title: Anthropic says these topics are too dangerous to let its Fable 5 model talk about
articles.description: New frontier model refuses cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry queries.

articles.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for instant voice-to-voice translation
articles.description: Voice translations preserve speaker's tone, pacing, pitch—with SynthID watermarks for security.

articles.title: NASA assigns crew for Artemis III, sets aggressive timeline for flying it
articles.description: "Artemis III will be an extraordinary demonstration of what is possible."

articles.title: Screwworms in US: Human risk is low—but they can burrow through your skull
articles.description: The chances are low, but not zero.

articles.title: One day after discovery, Meta pulls facial recognition code from its smart glasses
articles.description: Meta won't say why or whether it's coming back.

articles.title: Drone boat picked up downed US Army helicopter pilots—a first for sea rescues
articles.description: US Navy’s Task Force 59 achieved the drone rescue at sea near Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: High-severity vulnerability in Linux caused by a single faulty character
articles.description: Use-after-free bug can be exploited to evade sandbox defenses.

articles.title: Gold isn’t inert, it just has bodyguards protecting it
articles.description: Individual gold atoms move around to form oxidation-proof structures.

articles.title: Here's Audi's next Q7 SUV and US-only SQ7, now with an RS V8
articles.description: At night, the car projects its turn signals onto the road to alert other road users.

articles.title: Apple says its AI is still private, even when it's running on Google's servers
articles.description: Some models run in Google's cloud, but without giving Google any kind of access.

articles.title: First Drive: The 2027 Rivian R2 entirely changes the EV game
articles.description: Rivian's second EV is the sub-$60,000 R2, and it was worth the wait.

articles.title: FCC lifts looming deadline for Amazon Leo satellite broadband constellation
articles.description: The waiver "serves the public interest by promoting a second large satellite broadband constellation."

articles.title: Tests suggest Russian satellites can jam GPS on a continental scale
articles.description: Mystery of GPS interference across Europe raises questions about Russian motives.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI投入与工具争议**：企业每月为每位员工平均花费7500美元在AI上，但研究指出记忆工具可能反而降低AI模型性能；同时，网络安全研究人员对Anthropic的Fable模型的安全护栏表示不满。
2. **SpaceX与Starlink动态**：印度政府在SpaceX IPO前对Starlink的批准态度转冷；SpaceX的IPO被描述为依赖三大硬科技“登月计划”推动。
3. **企业AI与创业融资**：VivaTech 2026将聚焦企业AI；Datadog前员工创立AI编程公司Niteshift，旨在打破大厂锁定；Jedify获2400万美元融资，帮助企业为AI代理提供业务上下文。
4. **娱乐与平台更新**：Netflix在亚洲扩展改版移动应用并加强儿童游戏业务；Pinterest整合亚马逊店铺功能，押注创作者经济；Snapchat限制16岁以下用户仅能与好友分享Spotlight。
5. **数据安全与AI应用**：ServiceNow因漏洞导致部分客户数据暴露；华纳音乐收购AI归属初创公司Sureel AI；Decart发布可模拟数小时逼真驾驶的世界模型；Zest推出基于真实就餐数据的餐厅发现应用。

**今日最值得关注**：企业AI投入持续升温，但技术安全、数据隐私与模型局限性问题同步凸显，行业正从狂热转向更审慎的落地阶段。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **多语言语音智能体与开源模型进展**：Hugging Face发布针对代码混合语音的ASR基准测试，评估语音智能体处理双语用户的能力；Cohere推出面向开发者的首款模型“North Mini Code”；开源社区支持基于强化学习的OpenEnv智能体训练框架。
2. **AI Agent与平台工具创新**：通过链式调用Hugging Face Spaces构建3D巴黎画廊的Agent案例；GitHub CI可迁移至Hugging Face Jobs执行；设计Agent优化的hf CLI工具以高效操作Hub。
3. **企业级AI安全与多模态能力**：英伟达推出Nemotron 3.5内容安全模型，支持可定制的多模态安全功能，适配全球企业AI部署。
4. **OpenAI生态扩展与行业应用**：LSEG利用OpenAI模型规模化可信AI决策；Nextdoor和Notion工程师通过Codex实现无限制开发；OpenAI发布经济研究交换平台，并提交S-1草案推进上市进程。
5. **AI产业政策与普惠愿景**：OpenAI提出“智能时代工业政策”及“惠及所有人的计划”，强调AI发展的社会效益与包容性。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：多模态安全模型与Agent工具链的突破，标志着AI正从单一功能向复杂场景的自主协作与安全可控演进。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **中国科技与产业自主化加速**：计划投入2950亿美元建设以国产芯片为核心的AI数据中心，并启动全球首个风能水下数据中心；比亚迪目标五年内成为全球最大车企，宇树科技有望主导全球机器人产业；中国批准首款侵入式脑机接口芯片，展现从电池、医药到高铁、AI的全面领先地位。
2. **对外关系与地区安全**：中国重新调整对朝关系，以应对核问题沉默带来的地区平衡变化；台湾考虑效仿美国限制对华AI芯片出口；欧盟指责中国间谍网络扩张，并称中国化工产业冲击欧洲市场，布鲁塞尔计划干预。
3. **国内治理与监管**：中国加强无人机管控，使大量用户回归合规飞行；户籍制度改革仍面临顽固阻力，进展缓慢。
4. **军民融合争议**：五角大楼指控阿里巴巴、百度、比亚迪、宇树科技等企业支持中国军事发展，引发国际关注。
5. **新兴经济现象**：AI热潮意外带动中国人造钻石产业崛起，成为全球市场赢家。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：中国以2950亿美元规模的自主芯片AI基础设施计划为核心，全面加速科技自主与产业升级，同时在国际关系与监管领域面临多重博弈与挑战。
